mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment reduces aggregate disposable income and consumer spending, thereby lowering corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for 68% of US GDP
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE vs 20-day forward returns is positive (>0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean return in periods of rising UNRATE exceeds that in falling UNRATE periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the neutral rate proxy of 3.0%, monetary policy becomes contractionary, raising the discount rate on equity cash flows and compressing valuations over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:R-star estimates range 2.5-3.0% real, with 2% inflation implies 4.5-5.0% nominal
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in FEDFUNDS > 3.0% periods are positive at least 55% of the time, or average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and payroll employment signal robust consumer demand and income growth, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and PAYEMS above their respective 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: When both signals are positive, the proportion of negative 20-day forward returns exceeds 40%, or the mean return in such periods is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During NBER-defined recessions, declining industrial production indicates deepening economic contraction, which further depresses equity valuations and leads to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO contracts in every NBER recession since 1960
failure_condition: During recessionary months, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base through higher aggregate demand, which supports a rising equity market over longer horizons.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is negative or not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over the backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over three months signals a labor market shock, which triggers downward earnings revisions and negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.5 pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Sahm rule triggers at 0.5 pp increase, historically recession signal
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following a trigger, the proportion of positive returns exceeds 50%, or the mean return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve raises rates while housing starts simultaneously decline, monetary tightening is actively suppressing interest-rate-sensitive sectors, indicating economic weakness that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase and HOUST month-over-month decrease
failure_condition: In quarters following the joint trigger, 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPI year-over-year below 2.5%), a federal funds rate cut of at least 25 basis points signals accommodative policy that reduces borrowing costs and lifts equity valuations over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by at least 25bp in one month
failure_condition: Within low-inflation regimes, 5-day forward returns following a cut are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the average return is lower than in non-cut periods.
